calendar.matters:
Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Control Date)
Off Calendar Sep-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CORY J. BRIGGS OF THE BRIGGS LAW CORPORATION, TEL. NO. 1-619-221-9280 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEREMY M. GOLDMAN, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-554-6762 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' Motion for declaratory/injunctive relief and Writ Of Mandate - Motion for declaratory and injunctive relief and petition for writ of mandate is denied. The court will deny Plaintiffs' motion and petition on two grounds. First, the Plaintiffs have failed to establish that the Defendants withheld documents from Plaintiffs and violated their duty under Gov't Code 6253.1(a) to assist Defendants in their attempt to request and to access campaign finance contribution documents. "[A]n agency is not required to have 'clairvoyant capabilities' to discover the requester's needs." Hudgins v. I.R.S. (D.C. Cir. 1985) 620 F.Supp. 19, 21. In their request, Plaintiffs made no mention of campaign finances or campaign contributions. The Mayor's Office understood the request to refer to records of meetings with elected officials and gifts and donations received by elected officials, which made sense because it is the Mayor's Office's responsibility to keep track of that information. Furthermore, because the Ethics Commission publishes campaign finance filings on the internet for all to view and download, the Mayor's Office had no reason to believe Plaintiffs were trying to obtain the campaign finance contribution records through their office. See Kowalczyk v. Dep't of Justice (D.C. Cir. 1996) 73 F.3d 386,389. Contrary to Plaintiffs' position, the Board's communications with Plaintiffs' counsel, as provided in Exhibit G, do not support the allegation that the Board understood the request to refer to campaign finance contributions. Additionally, Mayor Lee had no duty under CPRA to provide his personal copy of the Form 460. See Gov't Code 6253 subd. (e). Thus, Defendants responded to Plaintiffs' request in compliance with the CPRA. The second reason for denying Plaintiffs' motion and petition is that the court already granted Defendants' motion to strike paragraphs 13A and 14 complaint. Because the Plaintiffs may no longer litigate the issues raised in paragraphs 13A and 14 of the complaint, they no longer have a basis to seek relief for a CPRA violation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
